title: 1.1: Tests that account linking succeeds when SimpleLogin user and Partner user have the same email address
1.2: Tests that account linking persists the association when linking accounts with same email
1.3: Tests that account linking succeeds when SimpleLogin user has a different email than the Partner account
1.4: Tests that account linking persists the association when linking accounts with different emails
1.5: Tests that account linking succeeds when Partner account is already linked to a different SimpleLogin user (same email scenario)
1.6: Tests that previous user is unlinked when Partner account is re-linked to new user (same email scenario)
1.7: Tests that account linking succeeds when Partner account is already linked to a different SimpleLogin user (different email scenario)
1.8: Tests that previous user is unlinked when Partner account is re-linked to new user (different email scenario)
1.9: Tests that account linking handles uppercase email addresses in the request
2.1: Places the account linking integration tests in an appropriate test file
3.2: All tests follow the test naming pattern test_<descriptive_name>
3.3: All tests use the flask_client fixture for database access
4.1: Tests use existing helper functions for creating test users and link requests
